By navigation to the details of the Repair Object the information about the Availability of the object can be checked: the Availability Status is changed to Received by Repair Center (the logistics status was “Delivery to Repair Center Pending” of the repair object in the previous step). This new logistics status of the repair object means that the inbound delivery was created against the Customer Returns Order and goods receipt was posted for the object in the background.

In the Transactional History it is showed that the Returns Order and the Repair Order created in this step are linked to the In-House Repair Order.

Step 3: Plan and perform repairs in the Repair Order

Once the precheck is finalized the Customer Service Representative can navigate to the Repair Order from the Transactional History and can start to plan the repair of the object. When they switch to Edit mode they are able to add service products are required to repair and bill the equipment. The item category Execution Order Item is assigned to the items in the Advanced Execution Process.

Customer Service Representative can also maintain or change the billing relevance under Billing in Repair Order for the execution order items. The entered billing relevance, fixed price billing or resource-related billing effects how the execution item is billed.  If Itemized Billing or Summarized Billing is maintained, a DIP (dynamic item processor) has to be also entered.

Once the details are defined in the Repair Order the follow-up action can be triggered:

If it is required to specify the repair effort by means of diagnosis and start to plan the diagnoses, the action Trigger Diagnosis Planning needs to be selected. After this action is triggered the status of the repair objects is changed to Diagnosis: Plan.

If it is required to repair the objects immediately and plan the repair, the action Trigger Repair Planning needs to be selected. In this case the status of the repair objects is changed to Repair: Plan.

Once the follow-up action is triggered, the status of the Repair Order is changed to In Process and the repair objects appear in the Plan Repairs app.

(If the process has to be cancelled the action Set Repair Order to Completed has to be selected. After the Repair Order is completed the status of the repair objects is changed to Bill.)

After the follow-up action Trigger Repair is selected the status of the repair objects is changed to Released.

When the Repair Order is saved the Maintenance Order creation is triggered automatically for each Execution Order Item in the Advanced Execution Process.

After the Maintenance Order is created the repair is planned in details and performed in the Maintenance Management. The execution status in the Execution Order Item shows the current status of the repair process in the Maintenance Management.

Once the status of the Maintenance Order is set to Technically Completed (TECO), the status of the related Execution Order Item is changed to Completed and the execution status is changed to Execution Done.

If the status of all items in the Repair Order is set to Completed, the status of the repair objects is changed to Bill and the billing process can be started.

Step 4: Prepare for Billing

If billing relevance of execution order items is set to Billing on Completion the Fiori app Prepare for Billing is used to create Billing Document Request for the billing-relevant items.

In this step the Customer Service Representative triggers the billing for repairs that was performed in In-House with Advanced Execution process and complete the in-house repair process for the repair objects.

When the Customer Returns Order exists and the repair objects were received by the repair center, an Outbound Delivery is created first to send back the repair objects back to the customer.

In this case the repair objects are displayed under To Be Returned in the Prepare for Billing app. Under the Decide screen in the repair object details the delivery can be created by the Customer Service Representative. By clicking on the Outbound Delivery the delivery document is generated by the system automatically in the background.

After creation the Outbound Delivery is displayed in the Transaction History:

The next step is to be release the repair objects for Billing under To Be Released for Billing in the same app:

The items can be released for billing if the following prerequisites are fulfilled:

  • The status of the repair objects is Bill
  • The status of the Repair Order is Completed and the execution status is Execution Done
  • The status of the related Maintenance Order is Technically Completed (TECO)

Once the Release for Billing is chosen the system generates the Billing Document Request for the selected repair objects in the background. The billing-relevant transaction data that is stored in the Billing Document Request is transferred to Sales Billing where the final Invoice is created.

The Billing Document Request is created for the Repair Object is displayed in the Transaction History:

(If billing relevance of execution order items is set to Itemized Billing or Summarized Billing the Fiori app Create Resource-Related Billing Request – Service Order is used to create the Debit Memo Request. Once the Debit Memo Request is created it is displayed in the Transaction History for the Repair Object. As in the case of Billing Document Request the billing relevant data is stored in the Debit Memo Request and transferred to Sales Billing where the final Invoice is created.)

When Outbound Delivery and the Billing Document Request are created, the status of the Repair Object can be set to Completed.

Once the status of all Repair Objects in the In-House Repair is set to Completed in the Prepare for Billing app, the status of the In-House Repair is changed to Completed automatically.

After that any In-House Repairs can no longer be edited.

 

The following apps were used in In-House Repair with Advanced Execution:

Manage In-House Repairs

Perform Prechecks

Process Repair Quotations

Plan Repairs

Perform Repairs

Prepare for Billing

Manage Repair Quotation

Manage Repair Orders

Manage Repair Confirmations

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Mass download of electronic fiscal documents
entry.content:
Versão em português aqui / Versión en español aquí 

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released a new functionality, the DF-e Mass Download.

Now, you can download many XML files of approved electronic fiscal documents (NF-e, CT-e, and NFS-e), including their events, for auditing purposes. The electronic fiscal document files are provided in a compressed file in tar.gz format.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: DF-e Mass Download

The new functionality delivers the following features:

  • The DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) transaction, or the DF-e Mass Download app for cloud users, provides several parameters that enable you to select the electronic fiscal documents according to your needs.

  • Download of many XML files from approved documents from specific company codes, business places, or CNPJ/CPF numbers and different electronic fiscal documents.

  • Better performance to download a high number of XML files.

  • Preview a summary of documents that will be downloaded in a specific group, classified by company codes, business places, and partners, based on filter selection.

  • The shortcut for the DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) transaction in the Goto menu of the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

  • Integration of the Usage Analytics dashboard with the downloaded files.

  • The NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction is restricted to downloading less than 500 documents.

Implementation

You can implement these changes by following the instructions on the SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3493359
  • SAP Note 3493360

If you use the SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP systems:

  • You can control access to the mass download functions using the Business Place Authorization Object for DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MBUPLA) authorization object. For further details regarding this object, refer to the authorization object documentation available within the system through the Authorization Object Maintenance (SU21) transaction.

  • You need to schedule the NF-e Mass Download Job Orchestrator (NFE_MASSD_JOB_REPORT) to run according to your business needs.

For more information about the DF-e Mass Download, refer to the following SAP Help Portal page:

Outbound Invoices > Mass Download

Integration with Usage Analytics

In the Usage Analytics dashboard, you find the mass downloaded files under Outbound Metric Name. In this metric, each group totals 15 thousand documents. And each group of documents is considered 1 processed document in the metric. Refer to the following scenarios as examples:

  • If you download 10 thousand documents, you will be charged for one group of 15 thousand documents. In other words, 1 document in the licensing metric.

  • If you download 25 thousand documents, you will be charged for one group of 15 thousand documents for the first 15 thousand documents and another group for the remaining 10 thousand documents. That is, 2 documents in the licensing metric.

  • If you download 50 thousand documents, you will be charged for four groups of 15 thousand documents each. That is, 4 documents in the licensing metric.

For more details about the Usage Analytics dashboard and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil, you can check out the blog post explaining how to use this tool.

Related blog posts

  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Download em massa de documentos fiscais eletrônicos
entry.content:
English version here / Versión en español aquí 

Olá pessoal,

O time de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil lançou uma nova funcionalidade, o DF-e Mass Download.

Agora, você pode baixar muitos arquivos XML de documentos fiscais eletrônicos aprovados (NF-e, CT-e, e NFS-e), incluindo seus eventos, para fins de auditoria. Os arquivos de documentos fiscais eletrônicos são fornecidos em um arquivo compactado no formato tar.gz.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: DF-e Mass Download

 

A nova funcionalidade oferece os seguintes recursos:

  • A transação DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD), ou o app DF-e Mass Download para usuários da nuvem, fornece vários parâmetros que permitem selecionar os documentos fiscais eletrônicos de acordo com suas necessidades.

  • Download de muitos arquivos XML de documentos aprovados de empresas específicas, locais de negócios ou números de CNPJ/CPF e diferentes documentos fiscais eletrônicos.

  • Melhor desempenho para baixar vários arquivos XML.

  • Visualização de um resumo dos documentos que serão baixados em um grupo específico, classificados por empresas, locais de negócios e parceiros, com base na seleção do filtro.

  • Atalho para a transação DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) no menu Ir Para da transação Monitor NF-e/CT-e (J1BNFE).

  • Integração do dashboard Usage Analytics com os arquivos baixados.

  • Transação Monitor NF-e/CT-e (J1BNFE) restrita a baixar menos de 500 documentos.

Implementação

Você pode implementar essas alterações seguindo as instruções nas SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3493359
  • SAP Note 3493360

Se você usa sistemas SAP S/4HANA ou SAP ERP:

  • Você pode controlar o acesso às funções de download em massa usando o objeto de autorização Business Place Authorization Object for DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MBUPLA). Para mais detalhes sobre esse objeto, consulte a documentação do objeto de autorização disponível no sistema por meio da transação Atualizar Objetos de Autorização (SU21).

  • Você deve agendar o job NF-e Mass Download Job Orchestrator (NFE_MASSD_JOB_REPORT) para ser executado de acordo com suas necessidades comerciais.

Para obter mais informações sobre o DF-e Mass Download, consulte a seguinte página do portal SAP Help:

Outbound Invoices > Mass Download

Integração com o Usage Analytics

No dashboard do Usage Analytics, você encontra os arquivos baixados em massa em Outbound Metric Name. Nessa métrica, cada grupo totaliza 15 mil documentos. E cada grupo de documentos é considerado 1 documento processado nessa métrica. Consulte os seguintes cenários como exemplos:

  • Se você baixar 10 mil documentos, será cobrado por um grupo de 15 mil documentos. Ou seja, 1 documento na métrica de licenciamento.

  • Se você baixar 25 mil documentos, será cobrado por um grupo de 15 mil documentos para os primeiros 15 mil documentos e outro grupo para os 10 mil documentos restantes. Ou seja, 2 documentos na métrica de licenciamento.

  • Se você baixar 50 mil documentos, será cobrado por quatro grupos de 15 mil documentos cada. Ou seja, 4 documentos na métrica de licenciamento.

Para mais detalhes sobre o dashboard do Usage Analytics e SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, você pode conferir o blog post que explica como usar essa ferramenta.

Blog posts relacionados

  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, xml files mass download, massdownload, download em massa, baixar em massa, df-e, documentos fiscais eletrônicos, electronic tax documents, electronic fiscal documents, factura electrónica, facturación electrónica, cumplimiento de documentos, nota fiscal electrónica, xml descarga masiva de archivos, descarga masiva, descarga en masa, bajar en masa, documentos tributarios electrónicos, documentos fiscales electrónicos, nfe_massd_main_screen, nfe_massd_filter, nfe_mbupla

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Descarga masiva de documentos fiscales electrónicos
entry.content:
English version here / Versão em português aqui

 ¡Hola a todos!

El equipo de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil ha lanzado una nueva funcionalidad, el DF-e Mass Download.

Ahora, puede descargar una gran cantidad archivos XML de documentos fiscales electrónicos aprobados (NF-e, CT-e, y NFS-e), incluidos sus eventos, para fines de auditoría. Los archivos de documentos fiscales electrónicos se proporcionan en un archivo comprimido en formato tar.gz.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: DF-e Mass Download

 

La nueva funcionalidad ofrece los siguientes recursos:

  • La transacción DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD), o la app DF-e Mass Download para usuarios de cloud, proporciona varios parámetros que le permiten seleccionar los documentos fiscales electrónicos según sus necesidades.

  • Descarga de muchos archivos XML de documentos aprobados de sociedades, lugares de negocio, números de CNPJ/CPF, y diferentes documentos fiscales electrónicos.

  • Mejor desempeño para descargar una gran cantidad de archivos XML.

  • Vista previa de un resumen de los documentos que se descargarán en un grupo específico, clasificados por sociedades, lugares de negocio e interlocutores comerciales (partners), según la selección de filtros.

  • El acceso directo para la transacción de DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MASS_DOWNLOAD) en el menú Ir a de la transacción de NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE).

  • Integración del dashboard Usage Analytics con los archivos descargados.

  • Transacción de NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) restringida a la descarga de menos de 500 documentos.


Implementación

Puede implementar estos cambios siguiendo las instrucciones de las SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3493359
  • SAP Note 3493360

Si utiliza los sistemas SAP S/4HANA o SAP ERP:

  • Puede controlar el acceso a las funciones de descarga masiva mediante el objeto de autorización Business Place Authorization Object for DF-e Mass Download (NFE_MBUPLA). Para obtener más detalles sobre este objeto, consulte la documentación del objeto de autorización disponible dentro del sistema a través de la transacción Actualización de Objeto de Autorización (SU21).

  • Debe agendar el NF-e Mass Download Job Orchestrator (NFE_MASSD_JOB_REPORT) para que se ejecute de acuerdo con las necesidades de tu negocio.

Para obtener más información sobre el DF-e Mass Download, consulte la siguiente página del portal SAP Help:

Outbound Invoices > Mass Download

Integración con Usage Analytics

En el dashboard de Usage Analytics, encontrará los archivos descargados en masa en Outbound Metric Name. En esta métrica, cada grupo suma 15 mil documentos. Y cada grupo de documentos se considera 1 documento procesado en la métrica. Consulte los siguientes escenarios como ejemplos:

  • Si descarga 10 mil documentos, se le cobrará por un grupo de 15 mil documentos. En otras palabras, 1 documento en la métrica de licencias.

  • Si descarga 25 mil documentos, se le cobrará por un grupo de 15 mil documentos por los primeros 15 mil documentos y por otro grupo por los 10 mil documentos restantes. Es decir, 2 documentos en la métrica de licencias.

  • Si descarga 50 mil documentos, se le cobrará por cuatro grupos de 15 mil documentos cada uno. Es decir, 4 documentos en la métrica de licencias.

Para obtener más detalles sobre el dashboard de Usage Analytics y SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil, puede consultar la publicación que explica cómo usar esta herramienta.

Publicaciones relacionadas

  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

Puede registrarse para recibir alertas por correo electrónico cuando se publiquen o actualicen nuevas SAP Notes en los componentes que se enumeran a continuación; solo siga las instrucciones del SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use la herramienta regulatory change manager para averiguar si se planea una solución para un cambio legal. Siga las instrucciones del SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 y a la página regulatory change manager para usar esta herramienta.

¿Te ha parecido útil este blog? Si es así, haga clic en “like” (kudos), compártelo con tus colegas y sígueme para ver contenido similar. No dude en dejar sus preguntas en la comunidad SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Y lea publicaciones de blog similares en blogging space SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

No olvide seguir la tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) y la comunidad SAP Document and Reporting Compliance para tener conocimiento de las últimas noticias de NF-e.

Gracias y saludos,
Thiago

Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, xml files mass download, massdownload, download em massa, baixar em massa, df-e, documentos fiscais eletrônicos, electronic tax documents, electronic fiscal documents, factura electrónica, facturación electrónica, cumplimiento de documentos, nota fiscal electrónica, xml descarga masiva de archivos, descarga masiva, descarga en masa, bajar en masa, documentos tributarios electrónicos, documentos fiscales electrónicos, nfe_massd_main_screen, nfe_massd_filter, nfe_mbupla

entry.title: Expert Talk: Inspection Checklist in Maintenance Management – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Discover the power of the Inspection Checklist solution in Maintenance Management with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This Expert Talk video provides a comprehensive walkthrough of the solution, demonstrating its seamless integration into the maintenance management process including maintenance order planning, checklist creation, transfer to technicians, recording checklists, closing checklists, and maintenance order completion based on the 2023 FPS01 release with lab previews for FPS02. Learn how the solution simplifies recurring inspections, triggers follow-up actions, and offers benefits such as increased efficiency, improved quality, and compliance with legal requirements.

Our Expert Talk video features Joerg Medack, responsible SAP Product Owner for the Inspection Checklist solution, Siu Loon Khuu, responsible for the plant maintenance application at Swiss railway company BLS AG plus speaker of DSAG topic group for Inspection Checklist, and Gerhard Welker, Product Expert at SAP.

In this insightful discussion hosted by Fernanda Rodrigues, the experts will take you on a deep dive into the concept, applications, and benefits. The video also brings a customer’s experience, with the company BLS AG sharing its experience on using the Inspection Checklist solution.

Watch the video now:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PIK1GERmgx8

Thanks for watching this video!

 

Details

Table of Contents

  • Introduction
  • DSAG Handout
  • Master Data
  • Maintenance Order Planning
  • Creating Checklists
  • Transfer to Technician
  • Recording Checklists
  • Closing Checklists
  • Maintenance Order Completion
  • Short Cut with Emergency Work
  • Links and SAP Notes

 

Introduction

Initialy, the inspection checklist process was introduced with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2021 FPS0. It was constantly enhanced so that the following content is based on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 with lab previews for FPS02.

You can use checklists to inspect and check assets. They are legally binding documents.
The inspection checklist process is an integrated end-to-end maintenance process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Besides the system customizing and configuration master data needs to be set up. The recurring process cycle starts with the maintenance order planning and then checklists get created and transfered for example to the technician. After the results recording the checklists get closed closed which can trigger automated follow-up actions like creating measuring documents. In the end the maintenance order gets completed and the inspection checklist cycle can start again. You can create inspection checklist templates (Quality Management inspection plans) for combinations of technical object attributes and recurring maintenance tasks such as monthly electrical check-ups. The Quality Management inspection lots are created by the system with reference to maintenance order operations and a technical object, such as a piece of equipment or functional location. You use a lean master data model by defining one inspection plan for a type of checklist for an object type used in the inspection process for all similar technical objects. This solution offers you reporting and analysis tools for checklist results regarding technical objects or inspection characteristics.
Is the inspection checklist process set up in your system, you'll benefit from a digitalized visual and functional inspection process. It will reduced efforts for operational execution and increases the transparency of the technical object condition.

The figure below provides a simplified overview of the end-to-end process. It can be used during the phase-based maintenance or the non-phase-based maintenance flow.

Figure 1: Inspection Checklist Process in Maintenance Management - Overview

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

DSAG Handout

The German SAP User Group DSAG offers a guide that provides lessons learned and best practices about the Inspection Checklist solution. The details are based on customizing, configuration and process flow.

You can download the German Version of the handbook here:
SAP Leitfaden – Checklisten in der Instandhaltung 

The international english version of the handbook is currently in the translation process and it is planned to be published.

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Master Data

From the standard solution enablement perspective the technical object must use the classification functionality with characteristics to determine the also classified inspection plan. The inspection plan is the inspection checklist template which contains all the inspection characteristics. These characteristics are relevant for the inspection process and can also be master data objects.

The master data figure below gives an impression how the technical object classification and an inspection plan with inspection characteristics can look like.

Figure 2: Master Data for Inspection Checklist Process

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Maintenance Order Planning

Let's assume that the order type and planning plant combination is activated for the checklist solution, then you can plan the inspection process. During the maintenance order creation a header reference object will be used and the operation will be planned with details like description or work and the checklist type. Depending on the customizing, the technical objects below the header reference object can be derived and placed in the object list while the order is initially saved. The new maintenance order system status OLGE (Object list generated) is set after the object list is derived.

The planned maintenance order below shows the operation with the description, a planned work and the checklist type on the left hand site. On the right hand site you see the automated derived technical objects from maintenance order header technical object and the system status OLGE.

Figure 3: 'Create/Change Maintenance Order' apps

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Creating Checklists

After the planning and approval of the maintenance order it can be released. If the automated checklist generation is activated during maintenance order release, then the checklists gets created. The technical objects from the object list and the operation-based checklist type will determine the inspection plan which creates the inspection lot for the inspection checklist process. The checklists are associated against the maintenance order operation. The new system status CLGE (Checklists generated) will be set to the maintenance order and the cross-app navigation function "Manage Inspection Lot" gets activated. This functions allows to review all generated checklists against the maintenance order.

If afterwards additional checklists should be generated, then a dedicated program or transaction can be used for it.

The following figure shows on the left hand site the new system status CLGE and the active cross-app navigation to the  "Manage Inspection Lots" app. The righ hand site lists the associated inspection lots from the order and the technical object for the inspection. In the footer area there is the program displayed that can be used for the after maintenance oder release checklist generation.

Figure 4: 'Change Maintenance Order' and 'Manage Inspection Lots' apps

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Transfer to Technician

After the checklists are beeing created there are some ways to transfer them to the technician.
In the figure below you can see two ways. On the left hand site, during the maintenance order processing the operation can be passed to the execution phase so that the checklists are available in perform maintenance jobs app as a backlog for the technician. Alternatively, you can send a checklist report with the work pack functionality by email. You can group job packs by the new introduced work pack grouping attribute operation checklist type. An example of a checklist report is shown on right hand site below which will be delivered with the 2023 FPS02 release.

Figure 5: 'Change Maintenance Order' and 'Perform Maintenance Jobs' apps

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Recording Checklists

The results recording of checklists and the time confirmation can be done in the perform maintenance jobs app. In the demo below you will see the assignment of a job to a technician, the results recording of three checklists and the time confirmation. Each checklist has his own inspection characteristics and a variety of specialized codes and numbers which are recorded.

Video: Recording Inspection Checklists

Video: Recording Inspection Checklists 

In addition, with the 2023 FPS02 release a new system check can be activated so that a final time confirmation is only possible if all required or processed inspection characteristics are valuated.

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Closing Checklists

After the results recording of checklists they can now be closed. By using a predefined code and assigned follow-up actions the usage decision can take place. In the figure below you see the new Fiori app "Manage Inspection Checklists" that provides insides and actions. The usage decision will accept or reject an inspection lot. The rejected inspection lot has triggered a new follow-up action for setting a new system status on the technical object. The new app and the new system status FLCO (Faulty Condition) will be delivered with the 2023 FPS02 release. 

Figure 6: 'Manage Inspection Checklists' and 'Asset Viewer' apps

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Maintenance Order Completion

The maintenance planner can review the maintenance order to validate if open main work still exists and if attachments from the checklist process are assigned. This can be triggered by a follow-up action from the usage decision code. When everthing is valid for the maintenance order then it can be technical completed. You can configure the combination of maintenance order type and planning plant in that way, that the technical completion is only possible if all assigned checklists are having an usage decision.

The figure below shows a review activity from a maintenance planner perspective. The maintenance order has no open main work and all checklists are closed, because one consolidated attachment is assigned.

Figure 7: 'Maintenance Order' object page with 'Operations' and 'Attachments' sections

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Short Cut with Emergency Work

With the 2023 FPS02 release it will be possible to assign a task list in the create maintenance request app. This task list assignment in combination with the Emergency Work processing context enables the enduser  directly of maintenance order creation with an exploded task list. The system generates the relevant checklists automatically if the customizing is set up accordingly. This feature enables the user to move directly to the execution phase after the initiation is done with the relevant planned task list information.

The figure below shows on the left hand site the used task list during the maintenance request creation and on the right hand site it shows the enhanced checklists appearance in the perform maintenance jobs app. 

Figure 8: Short Cut with Emergency Work

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Links and SAP Notes

  • Set up your SAP S/4HANA system for EAM Inspection ... - SAP Community
  • Overview of the Inspection Checklist Process | SAP Help Portal
  • DSAGNet - Veranstaltung 2023.01.13 (Fiori QM-Apps für die EAM-Checkliste)
  • SAP Leitfaden – Checklisten in der Instandhaltung 
  • DSAGNet - TG Checklists

SAP Notes

  • 3306826 - EAM Checklists: Business Feature EAM_IDMS
  • 3421917 - Inspection checklists and calibration
  • 3421907 - EAM Checklists: Data model and use of Inspection Points
  • 3279081 - EAM Checklists: Measurement document is not created (qualitative inspection characteristics)
  • 3432295 - EAM Checklists: Generation and Simulation issues - Status and Plant of QM inspection plan, Material assignment

Roadmap Items

  • Better handling of maintenance checklists with a new SAP Fiori app
  • Ability to execute checklists in the Perform Maintenance Jobs app
  • Integrated checklist-handling in Find Technical Objects and Find Maintenance Orders and Operations
  • Inspection checklist: work pack printing, confirmation check, follow-up actions for technical objects, and more

 

(🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Other SAP S/4HANA Cloud Enablement Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our Enablement team has created for you below:

 



Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Podcast

Subscribe to our podcast “Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud“! This podcast leverages the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product experts, partners and customers to address objects of interest by sharing product insights and project best practice. There is no customer success without product success and project success; we share all ingredients with you to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge.

 

openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud channel, so make sure you check them out. Recently we published brand new Microlearnings for Asset Management.

 

Your Voice Matters!

Provide us your feedback by commenting this video or get in touch with us via insides4@sap.com.

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, join our SAP S/4HANA topic page on SAP Community and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition topic page on SAP Community. The community brings together customers, partners, and SAP experts and has a clear mission: deliver an interactive community to engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore our ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and connect with experts to guide you through your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

More Information:

    • The Link Collection for Asset Management
    • SAP APM – Asset Strategy Overview
    • Release Blog Posts
    • YouTube Playlist for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • YouTube Playlist for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
    • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel
    • Former Early Release Series
    • Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Podcast
    • openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • SAP Community
    • Feature Scope Description
    • What’s New
    • Help Portal Product Page

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Innovation Highlights In The Latest Releases
entry.content:
In the current world, businesses cannot rely on a certain Unique Selling Point for their success. They must be proactive on all fronts to remain relevant, with technology being a pivotal pillar in driving organizational growth. The latest capabilities and technologies empower organizations to be a step ahead of the competition and utilize the opportunities presented by the global market.

Each release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition comes with a new set of capabilities bringing simplification, innovation, and automation to customers and their end users.

Stay updated with the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to maximize your investment!

Explore in this blog significant innovations, which have been delivered with the latest releases:

 

Table of Contents

Application Innovation Highlights In Line Of Businesses

  • Supply Chain

  • Service

  • Asset management

  • Sales

  • Finance

  • Manufacturing

  • Sourcing and Procurement

User Experience Innovations

SAP Business Technology Platform Innovations

Innovations With Business AI and Joule

Innovations For Sustainability

Other Resources

 

Application Innovation Highlights

Throughout the latest releases, we introduced a wide range of application innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, encompassing apps, APIs, functionality, and extensibility across all lines of business. These major innovations differentiate significantly from older SAP S/4HANA releases and SAP ERP.

Supply Chain

  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving simplifies communication between Transportation Management, Extended Warehouse Management, Stock Room Management, Inventory Management and Physical Inventory, and Logistics Execution application. Simplified SAP TM and SAP EWM Integration support mixed and multi-warehouse scenarios within one single freight order (FO) and helps users coordinate the process steps from the arrival of trucks at the gate to the final goods receipt in the warehouse.

  • With Supply Creation-Based Confirmation (SBC), the availability check in sales orders is significantly enhanced. SBC is typically used by manufacturing industries especially in “make-to-order / Assemble-to-order” scenarios when there is not enough stock in the warehouse for the requested product. With SBC, during the ATP check, you can create supply elements for a multilevel BOM – with resource scheduling and reservations on capacities - to immediately cover the sales order requirements when there is not enough supply available in the system.

  • The Greenhouse Gas Emission feature enables you to set CO2 emissions parameters for vehicle types to drive optimization and insights on sustainability.

A freight order with calculated emission data

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

Service

  • Service with Advanced Execution helps businesses to plan, execute, and bill services for complex assets or long-running services with multiple billing cycles. It supports service processes with longer service cycles and complex technical objects that require extensive planning and execution. The core characteristic of this process is the integration of the commercial aspects of Service with the planning and execution capabilities of Maintenance Management. The use of Maintenance Management capabilities means that you can use the task list that you are familiar with from the plant maintenance (PM) module of SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) and SAP S/4HANA, to represent complex maintenance operations and activities for industrial equipment, pieces of machinery, and other classes of assets.

  • The scenario lets you have a cost-plus commercial arrangement, where the service is charged to the customer based on the actual cost of activities plus a surplus or margin on top of the costs. That’s what dynamic item processor (DIP) or resource-related billing does.

  • A new way to deal with revenues and costs for service processes called Item-Based Accounting, which lets you have an insight into a real-time margin analysis before month-end financial closing and revenue recognition, leveraging the new Event-Based Revenue Recognition.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

Asset Management

  • Many innovations have been delivered to close the loop between asset strategy and asset execution. SAP Asset Performance Management (SAP APM) provides asset health insights from operational data and analytics and helps you craft cost-effective asset performance strategies for various asset classes.

  • SAP APM and S/4HANA work as a single system so that you can now directly reflect the strategic insights into the actual maintenance execution, enabling your maintenance teams to carry out different maintenance strategies appropriate for the asset criticality and class for optimal reliability and maintenance costs, and to measure efficiency and effectiveness of maintenance programs for continuous improvement.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

Sales

  • Low-touch sales order processing increases the sales team’s efficiency and productivity. One example is the automatic creation of sales orders from unstructured data, like purchase orders in PDF or Image formats, with the combined usage of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP Build Process Automation, and Document Information Extraction service, part of SAP AI Services.

  • The sales representative can import documents in XLSX format to create sales orders, sales quotations, sales contracts, or scheduling agreements.

  • The new Advanced Inter-company Sales process allows two affiliated companies to participate in a single inter-company process, e.g. a local sales office sells to a customer and the producing company delivers the goods directly to the customer or a local sales organization sells to a customer and a central warehouse delivers the goods.

Process Flow of Advanced Intercompany Sales

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

Finance

  • The new Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA), which provides a harmonized architecture for ledgers and currencies, has been introduced. UPA provides a foundation for calculating and posting values per ledger and currency along end-to-end processes.

  • UPA is also the baseline for future innovations in Finance such as Value Chain Analysis and Transactional Carbon Accounting.

  • The integration scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Group Reporting, SAP Advanced Financial Closing, and SAP Analytics Cloud have been continuously enhanced to accelerate the digital transformation in Finance.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

Manufacturing

  • Material Requirements Planning (MRP) Live provides significant performance improvements, shorter planning cycles (multiple times a day), up-to-date supply and demand information on which to base decisions, matching demand and supply more efficiently, cross-plant planning, and being the foundation for predictive MRP.

  • Predictive MRP provides midterm planning solutions to influence capacity planning through MRP prediction, predicts and simulates capacity constraints based on current MRP results, simulating and resolving capacity and material gaps in a plant before they occur.

  • Demand-driven MRP helps to buffer inventory based on target levels in strategic locations, the replenishment is triggered based on the average consumption over the lead time, and specific MRP type controls the consumption-based planning through parameters for Qualified demand, Qualified supply, Safety stock, Lot size, Reorder point.

  • Collaborative MRP enables you to run material requirements planning in real-time and visualize results, visualize material bottlenecks, and resolve them with a simulative approach collaborate with suppliers on short-notice changes, and receive instant feedback through SAP Business Network for Procurement.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

Sourcing and Procurement

  • Introduced Lean Services - a new master data object “Product type” differentiates material and services (new material type SERV). Both can be mixed in a hierarchical structure, using Model Service Specifications. After the service has been confirmed, the flexible workflow can be used to approve the service entry sheets.
  • The new item category enhanced limit can be used for unplanned services. By leveraging data from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, purchasers can track the carbon footprint of the products being requested in purchase requisitions.
  • Many investments have also been made in Central Procurement and Product Sourcing. 

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

 

User Experience Innovations

Each SAP S/4HANA release brings around 300 SAP Fiori apps, supporting improved processes and intelligent use cases.

  • New My Home Page from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 as shown in User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise).
  • Share to Microsoft Teams as shown in User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise).
  • Spaces and Pages provide a business-meaningful hierarchy with multiple tile types, personalized sort through pinned spaces, and merging of spaces/pages for users in multiple business roles – as shown in Getting Started with Spaces and Pages and Merging Spaces and Pages.
  • Situation Handling use cases for reacting faster to business exceptions have grown rapidly from 20 in SAP S/4HANA 1909 to 125 SAP-delivered use cases in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. You can even create your own custom situations. Refer to the blog series How to Start with Custom Situations and Custom Situation Cases: Configure Your Own Use Cases.
  • Introduction of the new embedded analytical app type Review Booklet Review Booklet for Group Financial Statements (Version 2.0).
  • System information toolbar, so users making key user extensions or testing always know which system they are working in as shown in User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise).
  • Live Guided Tours when executing an app, Quick Tours that automatically appear for first-time users, and revision as shown in SAP Fiori: Tips & Tricks for Users of SAP S/4HANA.
  • Read-only review tools for sharing launchpad content and layout details with your audit team and other business stakeholders, such as SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – 3 simple use cases for Launchpad Content Aggregator.
  • Support for composite security roles in launchpad content and layout tools, including exposure of composite roles to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. Refer to SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Composite Roles in launchpad content and layout tools.

Screenshot of the New My Home Page available from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

 

SAP Business Technology Platform Innovations

Each release enhances integration through the SAP Business Technology Platform with seamless connectivity across SAP cloud solutions, AI use cases, augmented analytics, and extended custom options.

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition launchpad to provide a unified entry point across SAP S/4HANA, your SAP Cloud solutions, and your custom apps built on SAP Business Technology Platform as explained in What Is SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition?
  • SAP Mobile Start as a native entry point to your launchpad on Apple and Android devices, including smartphones, Apple smartwatches, and Apple Vision Pro, as shown in Apple Vision Pro is available – SAP Mobile Start V1.10 is ready.
  • SAP Task Center as a unified approvals inbox across workflows from SAP S/4HANA, SAP SaaS solutions, and custom workflows built with SAP Build Process Automation – refer to the SAP Task Center one-pager summary.
  • Intelligent Situation Automation to automate handling of common business exceptions as shown in Intelligent Situation Automation – The next big step in Digital Transformation.
  • Easy integration of SAP Analytics Cloud stories and augmented analytics capabilities.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

 

Innovations With Business AI and Joule

AI-powered processes and with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition will pave the way to a new world of accelerated business, faster decision-making, and multiplied productivity. 

Joule, the AI copilot that provides intelligent answers drawn from the wealth of underlying business data:

  • Revolutionize the interaction, making every touchpoint count and every task simpler.
  • Have more productive business interactions using natural language for informational, navigational, and transactional assistance.
  • Gain smarter insights grounded in underlying business data and enriched by business context. 

Drive growth and foster innovation with AI:

  • Achieve improved finance excellence with AI that helps optimize working capital and closing functions.
  • Support operational excellence through optimized manufacturing and asset performance and elevated product quality.
  • Deliver advanced intelligent order fulfillment by streamlining and automating the order fulfillment process.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

 

Innovations For Sustainability

With an upcoming wave of mandatory ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reporting regulations, SAP plans to deliver one solution to enable an auditable foundation for carbon reporting and analysis in state-of-the-art finance quality and related to the same existing financial dimensions which are used to steer the company and on which financial reporting is done today.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

 

Other Resources

For details on the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition innovations, please refer to the What´s New Viewer, to this blog about S/4HANA Cloud Release Highlights in 2023 and 2022, or to the SAP Community. Our roadmap for future releases evolves around the key themes of intelligent Cloud ERP, leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Sustainability.

( 🔙 Table Of Contents)

entry.title: Key User Extensibility and Developer Extensibility for Carbon Footprint Category Breakdown
entry.content:
#saveThePlanet requires drilling into the details: Carbon Footprint Category Breakdown is released. Read my Blog article on how to extend your applications easily with Key User and Developer Extensibility for #SAPS4HANA

Introduction

SAP S/4HANA provides features that allow to extend existing applications with carbon footprint data. Our team’s previous Blog post described how to extend existing applications with carbon footprints, on the example of Manage Purchase Order. This is meanwhile standard functionality of Purchase Requisition and Purchase Order in current SAP S/4HANA releases. Nevertheless, the Blog post is still relevant for on premises installations not yet on the newest release and furthermore also for applications not yet enabled with footprints.

Today we bring it to the next level with our latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 release which includes the latest Sustainability Integration Component. It is enabled by Scope Item 5IM “Product Footprint Management” since several years, specifically since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2108 and SAP S/4HANA 2021. We introduced new CDS views as listed here including:

  • List of greenhouse gas scopes I_GHGScope.
    A GHG scope defines where emissions come from, e.g. from your own factory, your suppliers, or your consumers to name some examples from Scope 1, 2 and 3.
  • List of greenhouse gas categories I_GHGCategory.
    A GHG category further subdivides the greenhouse gas scopes, for example Scope 3 into category 3.06 “business travel”, 3.12 “end-of-life treatment of sold products” etc.
  • Master Data Footprint Breakdown by GHG Category I_CO2eqFprntAvgGHGCat.
    This one is best explained stepwise:
    • SAP S/4HANA contains master data records, specifying for example a product from a plant, or a product from a plant delivered by a specific supplier.
    • The master data footprint is a record that relates to an existing master data record. It adds footprint information to this master data record.
    • The Breakdown by GHG Category splits the master data footprint into quantity components per GHG category.
  • Transactional Data Footprint Breakdown by GHG Category I_PFMTransDataCO2eqFprntGHGCat. The transactional data footprint is related to a transactional data record. For example, it computes the overall CO2e sum for a given purchase order for the sum of the list of items ordered.

Prerequisites

Learning Recommendation

For an introduction into the topic of footprints, see the training Climate Action with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. It starts with an overview of Climate Action offerings by SAP and then focuses on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management hands-on and explains master data, transactional data, footprint computation etc.

Before you continue with the content below, I recommend making yourself familiar with Key User Extensibility and Developer Extensibility if you are not familiar already. I recommend Learning Journey “Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud”, especially the chapters

  • Explaining Key User Extensibility and
  • Explaining Developer Extensibility

User Roles

Make sure you have the following business roles assigned in your system:

  • SAP_UI_FLEX_KEY_USER
  • SAP_BR_PURCHASER
  • SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC
  • SAP_BR_ADMIN_SUS_PFM

Key User Extensibility

Create Custom Field to Display the CO2e Category Breakdown in Your Purchase Requisition Item

Custom Fields is a tool for Key User Extensibility. Using this tool, a key user can create and edit custom fields for SAP tables, Core Data Service (CDS) views or OData services and expose them to the UI.

I now extend Purchase Requisition Item. It already shows the CO2e footprint, and I additionally insert GHG category breakdown by adding a Custom Field. The steps required are:

  • Select Adapt UI from your profile icon menu top right.
  • Select Create Custom Fields
  • The list of Custom Fields is pre-filtered with the Business Context for Purchase Requisition Item, as you can see below. Create your new field here of Field Type: Text. Select something meaningful for label and identifier. The Business Context is prefilled with: Procurement: Purchase Requisition Item (MM_PURREQN_ITEM). The result should look like the screenshot below.
  • Position the newly created field to the place you want it to be. In the screenshot below I dragged it into the General Information section.

Create Custom Logic

As you saw in step 3 in the screenshot above, the category breakdown is placed now in the UI, but it does not yet show any value. Now I add the logic to define the text value. Go to application Custom Logic, add a new implementation in the Business Context and Extension Point as shown in the screenshot below.

For the code, you can take the example below as a first draft. It composes the text for the GHG breakdown by calling our Calculation API, looping through the returned components, and doing basic error handling. It is good practice to limit the code to your user at first to try things out without interfering with others, adapt this in line 1 before you proceed.

 

 

 

IF purchaserequisitionitem-createdbyuser = 'CB9980004180'. "Martin Knechtel
    " get footprint calculation API
    TRY.
        purchaserequisitionitemchange-yy1_mk_pr_co2category_pri = 'CO2e category breakdown coming from my Key User Extensibility'. "our custom field receives a first initial assignment, helps you debugging
        CATCH cx_sy_conversion_overflow.
            INSERT VALUE #(
                messageid        = 'MEPO'
                messagetype      = 'E'
                messagenumber    = 250
                messagevariable1 = 'CO2e category breakdown exceeds size of Custom Field.'
            ) INTO TABLE messages.
    ENDTRY.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_calculation_api) = cl_supfm_tdfp_calc_api_factory=>get_factory( )->get_calculation_api( ).
        CATCH cx_supfm_tdfp_calc_api_factory INTO DATA(lx_supfm_tdfp_calc_api_factory).
            INSERT VALUE #(
              messageid        = 'MEPO'
              messagetype      = 'E'
              messagenumber    = 250
              messagevariable1 = lx_supfm_tdfp_calc_api_factory->get_text( )
            ) INTO TABLE messages.
            RETURN.
    ENDTRY.
    TRY.
        " calculate the CO2e Footprint for the purchase requisition item
        DATA(ls_calculation_result_ghg) = lo_calculation_api->calculate_ghg_with_log(
                      VALUE #( ( 
Product       = purchaserequisitionitem-material "e.g. 'BUTTER100'
Plant         = purchaserequisitionitem-plant    "e.g. '1010' 
Quantity      = purchaserequisitionitem-orderedquantity "e.g. '3'
UnitOfMeasure = purchaserequisitionitem-purchaseorderquantityunit "e.g. 'KG'
                               ) ) ).

      " in case a footprint for the item was successfully calculated, loop over the components
      DATA responseString TYPE string.
      IF ls_calculation_result_ghg-has_result = abap_true.
          LOOP AT ls_calculation_result_ghg-pfmfootprintcomponents REFERENCE INTO DATA(component).
            DATA(quantityRounded) = round( val = component->pfmfootprintquantity
                                           dec = 3 ).
            responsestring = |{ responsestring }{ component->pfmfootprintqtycomponenttype }: { quantityRounded } { component->pfmfootprintunit } /  |.
          ENDLOOP.
      ELSE.
        responsestring = |No CO2e scope categories found. Log: { ls_calculation_result_ghg-calculation_log->get_calculation_log_json(  ) } Input: | &&
            |{ purchaserequisitionitem-material },{ purchaserequisitionitem-plant },{ purchaserequisitionitem-orderedquantity },{ purchaserequisitionitem-purchaseorderquantityunit }|.
      ENDIF.
      responsestring = |{ responsestring } at { cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date(  ) }{ cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_time(  ) }|.
      purchaserequisitionitemchange-yy1_mk_pr_co2category_pri = responsestring.
    CATCH cx_supfm_tdfp_calc_api INTO DATA(lx_supfm_tdfp_calc_api).
      INSERT VALUE #(
        messageid        = 'MEPO'
        messagetype      = 'E'
        messagenumber    = 250
        messagevariable1 = lx_supfm_tdfp_calc_api->get_text( )
      ) INTO TABLE messages.
    ENDTRY.
ENDIF.

 

 

 

Returning to your Purchase Requisition Item you should now see the CO2e category breakdown similarly to the screenshot below.

 

Developer Extensibility

Above I introduced Custom Logic for the ABAP code to prepare your text to be shown in the UI. This was done with Key User Extensibility tools. Now I switch into Developer Extensibilty. Preparing code with ADT is more comfortable if the Custom Logic above gets extensive. Debugging is possible. You can also encapsulate part of the logic and call it from the extension point Custom Logic above. An example is shown in the screenshot below. Similarly to the code example above, it calculates a transaction data footprint from a master data footprint and prepares the calculated GHG component breakdown to a string. Here it prints it to the console, but I could also assign it to some custom field, as shown above.

In case there is no master data footprint found for the given product “BUTTER100”, an error with message number 4 is returned. As shown in the screenshot, the CM_SUPFM_TDFP_LOGGER message class was opened to retrieve the message text from the message number. This might indicate to the developer that no test data was created yet on the system and afterwards he can proceed debugging and developing. With that, I walked you through an end to end example for extending your application with sustainability features with Key User Extensibility and Developer Extensibility.

Disclaimer: I describe the work of many here and I am only one of the contributors in a great and dedicated team. We keep on rocking to deliver you the latest and greatest sustainability integration into SAP S/4HANA.

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hey Everyone!

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 The ABAP sessions at this year's SAP TechEd event will be centered around "Clean Core Extensibility powered by ABAP Cloud and Generative AI". Attendees can look forward to a range of lectures (incl. roadmaps) and jump-starts on clean core development and transformation with ABAP Cloud and GenAI for all SAP S/4HANA editions, both cloud and on-premise. Below is an overview of the SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 sessions related to clean core extensibility and ABAP Cloud. 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024



Keynotes

KEY100CEST & KEY100EDT | SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
[Executive Keynote] At SAP TechEd, experts, developers, IT leaders, and our SAP Community gather to learn, share, and build together. Hear firsthand from SAP Board Members Juergen Mueller and Muhammad Alam how innovations in SAP Build solutions, SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and other solutions across our end-to-end portfolio are revolutionizing the tech world.´
Speakers: 
      • Juergen Mueller, Chief Technology Officer, SAP
      • Muhammad Alam, SAP Product Engineering, SAP
Schedule: 
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 9:00 AM - 10:30 AM CEST [KEY100CEST]
      • 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 4:00 PM - 5:30 PM CEST [KEY100EDT]

DEV101A & DEV101B | Keynote recap + Community welcome
[Developer Keynote] Join the Developer Advocates as they welcome the SAP Community to SAP TechEd, provide a short recap of the key announcements, and share insights into what it means for developers. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 10:55 AM CEST [DEV101A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 5:30 PM - 5:55 PM CEST [DEV101B]

DEV100A  & DEV100B | Developer keynote [deconstructed]: Keeping the promise
[Developer Keynote Deconstructed] Hear from the developer advocates for a quick injection of news, recaps, and deep-dive demos covering various announcements from the event as well as other fun stuff. 
Speakers: Developer Advocates, SAP 
Schedule: 
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST [DEV100A]
      • 📅Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST [DEV100B]

Track Overviews 

AD100 – Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions
[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, discover how to seamlessly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth. 
Speakers: Dr Alexander Rother (SAP), Thomas Volmering (SAP), Björn Schulz (REWE digital GmbH)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

DT100 – Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
[Track Overview, 45 min] Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. Delve into the technological backbone of SAP's vision for cloud ERP, covering in-app extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, AI-powered automation, and the "suite-first" approach. Learn the benefits of a clean core and its role in enabling composability, empowering businesses to adapt and evolve, and get insights into this year’s cloud ERP track sessions.
Speakers: Jan Gilg (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 10:30 AM - 11:15 AM CEST

Lectures

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

AD105 – Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build 
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions. 
Speakers: Dr. Anne Keller (SAP), Tim Back (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

AD107 – Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP Developer capabilities
[Lecture, 25 min] Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks. 
Speakers: Dr. Jasmin Gruschke (SAP), Sebastian Baskovich (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛  7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

AD106 – Clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA with the latest ABAP Cloud features
[Lecture, 25 min] ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building cloud-ready business applications, services, and extensions on both SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP S/4HANA, helping ensure clean core compliance by definition. Learn about the latest features offered in ABAP Cloud and how generative AI can boost the development efficiency. 
Speaker: Volker Drees (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

DT201 – Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] The extensibility portfolio for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition consist both on-stack and side-by-side extension techniques. In this lecture you will learn how on-stack key user extensibility can be used to develop a custom business object together with an application in SAP Build Apps. 
Speaker: Dr. Thomas Schneider (SAP), Alda Dollani (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 2:30 PM - 2:55 PM CEST

DT200 – Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to manage your custom, on-stack extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with the ABAP Cloud development model following clean core principles. Find out about the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in a well-known, three-tier extensibility model and how to handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud. Get an outlook of upcoming generative AI support and hear our plans for ABAP test cockpit to support governance of your clean core developments.
Speaker:  Olga Dolinskaja (SAP), Thomas Fiedler (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:00 PM - 3:25 PM CEST

Jump-Starts

AD180 – Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud powered by Joule's ABAP Developer capabilities
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model supported by generative AI. You will also learn about features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only treeviews for displaying hierarchical data. 
ABAP Cloud is the comprehensive development model for building clean core compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, in the cloud and on-premise. 
Speaker: Carine Tchoutouo Djomo (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:30 PM - 7:55 PM CEST

DT280 – Building Clean Core Extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
[Jump-Start, 25 min] Learn how to speed up the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensions with ABAP Cloud using two new ABAP Development Tools (ADT) based generators for adding extension fields and for building custom UI services on top of business objects that have been released by SAP. 
Speaker: Andre Fischer (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 3:30 PM - 3:55 PM CEST

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024 and watch the replays of last year's events, SAP TechEd Virtual in 2023 and SAP's Devtoberfest in 2023.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: Custom Business configurations for S/4 HANA 2023 private cloud edition: Some tips & Tricks
entry.content:
Hi All,

As SAP moves towards clean core with various key user extension tools, a significant part of any customer project remains the use of custom tables to cater to very particular business requirements. Some common use cases can be fixed value tables which will maintained in each environment directly by the business team or some custom configuration tables which will maintained through a customizing request.

So in this blog post i hope to share my experience on working with custom business configuration app in S/4 HANA private cloud edition.

OVERVIEW

As per the best practices suggested by the SAP ABAP cloud development model, the maintenance of custom business configuration objects which was previously done using SM30 or SM54 should be moved to RAP based BC objects.

Below is the availability matrix of what is available today till S/4 HANA 2023 FPS01. The custom business configuration app is only available from S/4 HANA 2023 onwards

MAIN CONTENT

Prerequisites: The custom table on top of which the custom BC set has to be created should be a customizing type of table.

1. Create a Z se11 table of customizing type. To create the custom BC objects it's mandatory to add 3 fields in the table, refer to the screenshot below.

2. Once the table is created we go to ADT tools and try to generate the RAP objects. Select the business configuration management options to generate the necessary artifacts.

 

3. Provide the necessary names to the artifacts, the service definition, binding, transport object, behavior implementation and the underlying CDS views. The name of all the objects are auto-generated by the wizard, you can change it if you want it.

4. Once all these objects are created, we should be able to test it app locally, but before that we need to activate the Odata service. 

Note: By Default this a Odata V4 service, and it can't be published locally from ADT. Please use t.code /IWFND/V4_ADMIN to activate the service group.

5. Once the service group is published, your service binding will show a local endpoint which can be tested.

6. Lets go to the FIORI launchpad and open the custom business configuration app. Please note the steps for activating the app can be found by referring to the fiori app library.https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4579')/S28OP

7. Click on the row and it will open the table , which can then we edited to create new entries.

This concludes on how to create a basic Custom business configuration object.

Now time for some TIPS & TRICKS:

Saving without transport

1. All these tables by default expect a transport while saving. However there might be numerous cases where table has to maintained directly in production by the IT team or the business user. In that case you follow the below steps.

  • In transaction SOBJ, set "Current Settings" = true and "Transport" = "No Transport" in the header data of the generated transport object. Save your entries.

  • Edit the behavior definition, delete the action "SelectCustomizingTransptReq" and all validations "ValidateTransportRequest". Remove annotation "with additional save". Save and activate.

     

  • Edit the behavior implementation class:Delete the method "SelectCustomizingTransptReq".

  • Delete all methods "ValidateTransportRequest"

  • Remove all occurrences of "%ACTION-SelectCustomizingTransptReq"

  • clear the method "SAVE_MODIFIED", i.e. leave the method implementation empty.

  • Delete the variable "selecttransport_flag" and its occurrences in method "GET_INSTANCE_FEATURES" of local class LHC_<singleton entity>.

  • In the metadata extension of the singleton entity, remove the field TransportRequestID. Save and activate.

Select all entries on the UI

By default there is only a de-select all button, to select all the entries select the first and the last record of the selection by pressing SHIFT+A.

Please note a maximum of 200 entries are allowed at a time.

Transporting the custom business configuration object

All the ADT artifacts are already captured in a workbench TR during creation, however the service group activation and the service group need to be captured in a TR manually using /IWFND/V4_ADMIN t.code.

For S/4 HANA 2022

Since the custom business configuration app is not there for each maintenance object a custom FIORI app needs to be developed using BAS and deploy it to FIORI launchpad.

CONCLUSIONS:

Custom business configuration provides an alternative to classic GUI based SM30 views to maintain custom tables. As we move towards S/4HANA public cloud where GUI based transactions are not supported, RAP based apps will be the cornerstone for all customer implementations.

There is also a tutorial if you like to do it step by step in a Trial system.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-business-configuration-object.html

Hope you like this blog and provide your comments.

Feel free to reach out to me if you any questions

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Looking for learning materials on how to drive and implement SAP Business Network Supplier Portal?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering SAP Business Network Supplier Portal Cloud Private Edition Implementation and Adoption learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the core features and activities for SAP Business Network Supplier Portal implementation and adoption.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be equipped to drive and implement SAP Business Network Supplier Portal by applying the knowledge learned about integration between S/4HANA and SAP Business Network, buyer account management, transaction rules, and Trading Partner enablement.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: How to perform mass upload from an Excel file in a FIORI App in SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Part 2
entry.content:
Introduction

In continuation to my previous blog post (How to perform mass upload from an Excel file in a FIORI App in SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Part 1), in this blog post, I will showcase an easy way to extend a FIORI Elements-based OData V4 application in Business Application Studio (BAS) to add a custom action on the responsive table header of the list report page. This custom action will allow the user to open a dialog to browse local files and upload an Excel file. 

In this blog post, I will be creating a Fiori Elements-based application using the tools provided in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) and will be extending generated apps using App Extensions to trigger OData V4 operations (in this example, RAP Static Action 'fileUpload' to upload an Excel file and RAP Static Function 'downloadFile' to download an Excel template).

Prerequisites

  • SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) subscription and developer role in your respective BTP Subaccount.
  • Basic knowledge of performing App Extensions to extend generated FIORI Elements based apps.
  • Basic UI5 skill.
  • Use of File Uploader control.

Basic of OData V4 Operation

Function - Functions are operations exposed by an OData service that don't have side effects. Functions must return data and can include additional path segments. Functions are invoked using HTTP method GET.

Action - Actions are operation exposed by an OData service that can have side effects. Actions can return data but must not be composed with additional path segments. Actions are invoked using HTTP method POST.

Operation - Both Functions and Actions are operations that can return data. Operations are either bound to a resource (for example, an entity type), that makes them members of that instance type. Operations can also be unbound. Unbound operations are called as static operations (using “action imports” or “function imports”) since a static (unbound) operation can't be called directly.

The OData V4 model supports OData operations (Action Import, Function Import, Bound Actions and Bound Functions).

RAP - Non-Standard Operations

Non-standard operations are RAP BO Operations that provide transactional behavior and that are user defined and implemented. There are two kinds of non-standard operations.

  1. Action: User-implemented operations that change the data of a BO instance. They are self-implemented operations. Two main categories of actions can be implemented in RAP:

    1. Non-factory actions: Defines a RAP action which offers non-standard behavior. The custom logic must be implemented in the RAP handler method FOR MODIFY. An action per default relates to a RAP BO entity instance and changes the state of the instance. An action is related to an instance by default.
